title: CSharpDB SQL Compatibility
description: Feature-level compatibility for the CSharpDB SQL dialect in CSharpDB 4.1.0. This released snapshot separates current availability from roadmap intent and does not imply compatibility with another vendor dialect.

features.syntax: CREATE TABLE customer_orders (order_id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY)
limitations: An unquoted name must begin with a letter recognized by .NET or underscore and continue with letters, decimal digits, or underscores. Reserved keywords require quoting, NUL is rejected, and the maximum length is 128 characters.
deviations: Name resolution is ordinal case-insensitive on current catalog paths.

features.syntax: CREATE TABLE "order details" ("order id" INTEGER)
limitations: Core SQL tests cover reserved words, whitespace, and doubled-quote escapes through CREATE, INSERT, and SELECT. Names are limited to 128 characters and cannot contain NUL. A dedicated reopen/catalog proof and feature-specific ADO.NET, REST, gRPC, and EF query/migration round trips are not yet linked; the EF proof covers identifier generation only.
deviations: Double quotes delimit identifiers and a doubled double quote escapes a quote. Name resolution remains ordinal case-insensitive rather than introducing case-sensitive quoted names.

features.syntax: CREATE TABLE users (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL)
limitations: This row covers primitive columns, nullability, and a single-column primary key; defaults, checks, and composite primary keys are separate rows.

features.feature: Basic SELECT with projection and filter
features.syntax: SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = @id
limitations: Advanced expressions and generated provider shapes are qualified separately.

features.feature: Hand-written INNER JOIN with an equality predicate
features.syntax: SELECT o.id, c.name FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customer_id = c.id
limitations: This row does not qualify every join type, predicate shape, or EF-generated join.

features.syntax: WITH active AS (SELECT id FROM users WHERE enabled = 1) SELECT * FROM active
limitations: Recursive evaluation is not claimed by this row.

features.syntax: SELECT id FROM current_users UNION SELECT id FROM archived_users
limitations: Current execution materializes both inputs before duplicate elimination.
deviations: UNION removes duplicates; duplicate-preserving UNION ALL is qualified separately.

features.syntax: SELECT id FROM current_users UNION ALL SELECT id FROM archived_users
limitations: Core tests cover duplicate and NULL preservation, first-branch column names, numeric promotion, incompatible-type rejection, parenthesized operands, view serialization, outer LIMIT short-circuiting, and cancellation cleanup. Feature-specific ADO.NET, REST, gRPC, and EF query proofs are not yet linked; INTERSECT ALL and EXCEPT ALL remain unsupported.
deviations: Branch types must match or have a supported numeric common type; incompatible branches fail before enumeration.

features.feature: Experimental ranking and aggregate window slice
features.syntax: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY created_at)
limitations: Experimental in-memory slice only: one compatible window specification per SELECT; ROW_NUMBER, RANK, DENSE_RANK, and non-DISTINCT COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, and MAX over direct source rows. Star and mixed-expression projections preserve public SELECT-list order and hide internal window slots. Explicit frames, named windows, nested windows, mixed ordinary aggregation/grouping, incompatible specifications, navigation functions, and spill-to-disk are rejected. Large inputs remain bounded only by available process memory, although long sort/evaluation loops poll cancellation.
deviations: Default frames are SQL-consistent: an unordered window covers the whole partition, while an ordered aggregate window uses peer-aware unbounded-preceding through current-row semantics.

features.syntax: status TEXT DEFAULT 'new'
limitations: The first slice accepts typed literal and NULL defaults only. Column references, parameters, functions including CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, subqueries, computed expressions, and EF DefaultValueSql remain rejected. Core SQL, ADO.NET metadata, direct/REST/gRPC schema transport, archive round trips, and EF create-table literal defaults have focused coverage.
deviations: Omitted columns and explicit DEFAULT apply the persisted literal; explicit NULL remains NULL and is checked independently against nullability.

features.feature: DEFAULT markers and DEFAULT VALUES in INSERT
features.syntax: INSERT INTO orders (status) VALUES (DEFAULT)
limitations: Core SQL covers INSERT DEFAULT VALUES and DEFAULT markers in value tuples against literal column defaults. Feature-specific ADO.NET, REST, gRPC, and EF query paths are not yet linked, and the available default expression shapes are limited by the literal-only column DEFAULT row.
deviations: An explicit NULL does not request a default; it remains NULL.

features.syntax: CONSTRAINT ck_total CHECK (total >= 0)
limitations: Checks must be deterministic and row-local. Parameters, functions, subqueries, and qualified references are rejected. Core SQL covers enforcement, persistence, catalog exposure, rename dependency repair, named ALTER add/drop, ADO.NET metadata, direct/REST/gRPC schema transport, archive round trips, and EF create-table checks.
deviations: A false result rejects the write, while NULL/UNKNOWN passes in accordance with SQL CHECK semantics. Enforcement is immediate.

features.syntax: amount INTEGER CONSTRAINT ck_amount CHECK (amount >= 0)
limitations: A column-scoped CHECK may reference only its own column and otherwise follows the deterministic row-local expression limits of table CHECK. Core SQL covers enforcement, persistence, catalog exposure, column/table rename repair, ADO.NET metadata, direct/REST/gRPC schema transport, archive round trips, and EF create-table checks.
deviations: A false result rejects the write, while NULL/UNKNOWN passes. Dropping a column with a CHECK dependency is rejected.

features.syntax: CONSTRAINT pk_order_lines PRIMARY KEY (order_id, line_no)
limitations: This row covers PRIMARY KEY declarations inside CREATE TABLE. A composite key is a logical constraint and does not infer integer identity behavior; the separately qualified standalone ADD/DROP behavior starts in 4.2.0.
deviations: One primary key is allowed per table and every participating value must be non-NULL.

features.syntax: CONSTRAINT uq_tenant_email UNIQUE (tenant_id, email)
limitations: SQL and EF migrations support named UNIQUE declarations during CREATE TABLE plus standalone ADD/DROP CONSTRAINT. A unique constraint cannot be dropped while a foreign key depends on it.
deviations: Rows containing NULL in any participating column do not collide with one another.

features.syntax: name TEXT NOT NULL
limitations: Core tests cover SQL INSERT rejection, shared direct InsertBatch validation, and sys.columns nullability. A narrow reopen proof and feature-specific ADO.NET, REST, and gRPC write tests are not yet linked.
deviations: NOT NULL is enforced independently from CHECK; CHECK may accept UNKNOWN while NOT NULL still rejects NULL.

features.feature: SQL three-valued comparison and logical semantics
features.syntax: NULL = 1; 1 AND NULL; 0 OR NULL
limitations: Direct evaluator tests cover NULL comparisons, NOT NULL, and the AND/OR truth-table cases exercised by CHECK enforcement. This row does not yet qualify every WHERE, JOIN, IN/NOT IN, aggregate, transport, ADO.NET, or EF-generated expression shape.
deviations: UNKNOWN is represented as NULL; logical operators short-circuit to FALSE or TRUE where SQL truth tables permit a determinate result.

features.syntax: parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES parents(id)
limitations: The referenced column must be an ordered primary or unique candidate key with a compatible type and collation. EF emits named table-level declarations and supports standalone add/drop migrations.
deviations: Enforcement is immediate; deferred validation is not supported.

features.syntax: parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES parents(id)
limitations: RESTRICT is immediate for scalar and composite keys; deferred constraints are a separate unsupported feature.
deviations: Omitting ON DELETE selects immediate RESTRICT.

features.syntax: parent_id INTEGER REFERENCES parents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
limitations: CASCADE is immediate for scalar and composite keys; deferred constraints are a separate unsupported feature.

features.syntax: FOREIGN KEY (parent_id) REFERENCES parents(id)
limitations: SQL and the EF provider support named table-level scalar and composite declarations plus standalone add/drop migrations.
deviations: Constraint validation is immediate and uses MATCH SIMPLE for nullable composite child keys.

features.syntax: FOREIGN KEY (order_id, line_no) REFERENCES lines(order_id, line_no)
limitations: The referenced columns must exactly match the order, types, and collations of a primary or unique candidate key. MATCH FULL, MATCH PARTIAL, deferred validation, and ON UPDATE actions are not supported.
deviations: MATCH SIMPLE is used: any NULL child component suppresses the parent lookup.

features.syntax: REFERENCES parents(id) ON UPDATE CASCADE
limitations: Every ON UPDATE action is rejected.

features.feature: Add a nullable column without a default
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN nickname TEXT
limitations: Computed values are not supported. Literal-default backfill and selected ALTER COLUMN metadata actions are qualified in separate rows.

features.feature: Add a column with a literal default and backfill existing rows
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'new'
limitations: The default must be one of the supported typed literal or NULL forms. Adding a required column without a value for existing rows is rejected. A failed rewrite makes either explicit transaction API rollback-only.
deviations: Existing rows are streamed through a shadow table root and receive the persisted default before the catalog root is swapped.

features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users DROP COLUMN obsolete_value
limitations: Indexed, constrained, trigger-, validation-rule-, or stored-view-dependent, primary-key, and last-column drops are rejected. Stored-view preflight is deliberately table-conservative and follows direct/transitive view chains rather than attempting column-level SQL rewriting. Deterministic fault-injection and process-interruption coverage remain follow-on work.

features.feature: Set or drop a literal column default
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN status SET DEFAULT 'new'
limitations: SET DEFAULT accepts only the literal default forms supported by CREATE TABLE; it changes future writes and does not rewrite existing values.

features.feature: Set or drop column NOT NULL metadata
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN email SET NOT NULL
limitations: SET NOT NULL scans existing rows and refuses the metadata change if any value is NULL. Bounded numeric type and TEXT collation rewrites are qualified in separate rows.

features.feature: Convert a dependency-free column between INTEGER and REAL
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE measurements ALTER COLUMN amount TYPE REAL
limitations: Only INTEGER-to-REAL and REAL-to-INTEGER are accepted. INTEGER values must remain within the exactly representable inclusive range -2^53 through 2^53; REAL values must be finite, integral, and within the signed 64-bit INTEGER range. An incompatible stored value leaves the original root and schema unchanged. The altered column cannot participate in a primary/unique key, ordinary index, or incoming/outgoing foreign key. The conservative preflight also rejects any view that references the table, any trigger owned by the table, a cross-table trigger that references the column, and an applicable validation rule. Affected-index rebuild and dependency rewriting are not implemented. TEXT/BLOB conversions remain unsupported. A persisted default must validate for the target type, so DROP DEFAULT and a later compatible SET DEFAULT may be required. The generated multi-command default/type/nullability sequence must execute in one migration transaction so a conversion failure restores every facet.
deviations: Successful conversion streams rows through a transactional shadow root, preserves each physical row ID and indexes on other columns, revalidates CHECK constraints, swaps the catalog root atomically, and persists across reopen. EF Core infers the old store type independently of the target model column and orders DROP DEFAULT, TYPE, SET DEFAULT, and nullability changes when those facets change together.

features.feature: Set or drop a TEXT column collation
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE customers ALTER COLUMN code SET COLLATION NOCASE
limitations: SET COLLATION and DROP COLLATION apply only to TEXT columns. Ready ordinary and unique SQL indexes that inherit the column collation are rebuilt; indexes with an explicit collation and indexes on other columns retain their roots. Primary/unique key constraints, incoming/outgoing foreign keys, full-text or collection dependencies, and non-ready indexes remain unsupported. The conservative preflight also rejects any view that references the table, any trigger owned by the table, a cross-table trigger that references the column, and an applicable validation rule. Existing rows are rechecked under the target collation, so a CHECK failure or newly colliding unique-index values reject the rewrite and leave every original root unchanged.
deviations: The metadata change builds detached table and affected-index roots, swaps their catalog payloads atomically in the active transaction, preserves each physical row ID, reclaims the replaced roots only after adoption, and persists across reopen. DROP COLLATION restores the default binary comparison behavior.

features.feature: Add or drop a named CHECK constraint
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE orders ADD CONSTRAINT ck_total CHECK (total >= 0)
limitations: Only named row-local deterministic CHECK expressions are accepted. Adding a constraint validates every existing row before the catalog is changed.
deviations: A CHECK expression that evaluates to UNKNOWN passes, matching CREATE TABLE CHECK behavior.

features.syntax: ALTER TABLE items ADD CONSTRAINT pk_items PRIMARY KEY (tenant_id, code)
limitations: Logical single-column and composite keys are added without a table rewrite after all existing rows pass non-NULL and uniqueness validation. One primary key is allowed per table. A single INTEGER primary key uses the physical row key: populated tables are rekeyed from validated non-NULL unique column values while every ready ordinary/unique SQL, constraint-internal, and foreign-key-internal index is rebuilt atomically. Full-text, collection, and non-ready indexes remain unsupported and reject the operation before mutation.
deviations: Logical keys use an engine-owned unique backing index. Adding any primary key makes its columns NOT NULL; a physical single INTEGER key also gains identity/default-value generation. Its high-water mark is not reduced and advances past the largest existing nonnegative key when that successor is representable.

features.feature: Drop a named or current primary-key constraint
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE items DROP CONSTRAINT pk_items; ALTER TABLE items DROP PRIMARY KEY
limitations: DROP CONSTRAINT requires the stored constraint name and is the normal EF Core generated form. DROP PRIMARY KEY targets the current key without a name and also handles legacy unnamed keys. A referenced key cannot be dropped unless an equivalent ordered UNIQUE candidate key remains for every dependent foreign key.
deviations: Dropping a logical key removes its engine-owned backing index. Dropping a physical single INTEGER key removes identity/default-value generation. Primary-key columns remain NOT NULL after either form.

features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users RENAME TO customers
limitations: This row does not claim arbitrary vendor-specific rename syntax. Renames reject stored-view, trigger, and validation-rule dependencies until those definitions can be rewritten atomically.

features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users RENAME COLUMN old_name TO new_name
limitations: Dependency repair is implemented for current indexes, keys, checks, and foreign keys. Stored-view, trigger, and applicable validation-rule dependencies are rejected until their definitions can be rewritten atomically; a dedicated successful-rename reopen proof is not yet linked in the 4.1.0 release snapshot.

features.syntax: ALTER TABLE users RENAME INDEX ix_users_name TO ix_users_full_name
limitations: Only ordinary SQL indexes can be renamed directly. Constraint-, foreign-key-, full-text-, and collection-owned indexes retain engine-managed names.
deviations: The physical index root and uniqueness behavior are preserved; existing prefix statistics for the old name are invalidated.

features.feature: Drop a foreign-key constraint by name
features.syntax: ALTER TABLE children DROP CONSTRAINT fk_children_parent
limitations: The named constraint must exist. Provider versions before 4.2.0 may have generated engine-owned names for create-time foreign keys; those stored names must be used in one-time drop migrations.

features.feature: Core sys tables, columns, indexes, views, and triggers catalogs
features.syntax: SELECT * FROM sys.columns
limitations: This row covers the existing core catalogs, not information_schema or future generalized constraint catalogs.
deviations: CSharpDB uses sys.* virtual catalogs and selected underscored aliases.

features.feature: Normalized built-in and callback function catalog
features.syntax: SELECT * FROM sys.functions
limitations: The registry centralizes names, aliases, kinds, arity, return metadata, determinism, and registered callbacks. Rich binder type signatures, generated documentation tables, and a broader conformance corpus remain follow-on work.
deviations: CSharpDB exposes this metadata through sys.functions and the sys_functions alias.

features.syntax: connection.GetSchema("Columns")
limitations: Supported collections are MetaDataCollections, Restrictions, Tables, Columns, Indexes, ForeignKeys, Views, CheckConstraints, KeyConstraints, and KeyColumns. Collection-specific restrictions are documented by the Restrictions collection.

features.syntax: reader.GetSchemaTable()
limitations: The schema table reports intrinsic result metadata including ordinal, CLR/provider types, nullability, sizes, key/unique/identity flags when known, and expression shape. The current binder does not carry reliable source lineage for every projected expression, so unavailable base catalog, table, and column names are deliberately returned as NULL instead of being invented.

features.feature: EF Core exact scaled-integer decimal foundation
features.syntax: modelBuilder.Entity<Invoice>().Property(i => i.Amount).HasPrecision(18, 4)
limitations: The provider maps decimal and nullable decimal to signed scaled INTEGER storage. The default is decimal(18,2); precision must be 1-18 and scale 0-precision. Exact round trips, parameters used with one facet mapping, equality/range comparisons, ordering, nullable values, and ordinary indexes are qualified. Excess fractional digits and precision overflow are rejected rather than rounded. Decimal keys, database defaults, generated values, custom store types, computed decimal expressions (including arithmetic, numeric casts, conditionals/coalescing, and aggregates), decimal collection/subquery Contains, cross-facet parameter reuse, comparisons with application-converter decimal mappings, model-mapped decimal functions, and precision/scale-changing migrations remain unsupported and fail explicitly. Complex properties are rejected until flattened mappings are qualified. Direct IMigrationsSqlGenerator calls with hand-authored AddPrimaryKeyOperation instances must pass the target model; model:null does not carry enough column metadata to identify a decimal mapping.
deviations: Raw SQL observes the scaled INTEGER representation rather than a native DECIMAL storage class.

features.feature: EF Core bounded direct inner joins
features.syntax: db.Parents.Join(db.Children, p => p.Id, c => c.ParentId, (p, c) => new { p.Name, c.Value })
limitations: Exactly one explicit Queryable.Join without a custom comparer is qualified between sources that normalize to direct mapped entity roots. The outer root may have a pre-filter; the inner root must be unfiltered because EF Core otherwise emits a derived JOIN target not accepted by CSharpDB's current table-reference grammar. Each side must use one direct nonnullable INTEGER-backed int, long, or int/long-backed enum property with compatible provider mappings. Qualified scalar or entity result projection plus post-join filtering, ordering, and Skip/Take are supported. Prior ordering or limits, source shapes that remain projected or derived after EF normalization, nullable/text/decimal/configured-converter/transformed/composite keys, custom comparers, chained joins, GroupJoin, SelectMany, DefaultIfEmpty, and left/right joins fail before command dispatch.

features.feature: EF Core embedded CRUD and bounded query translation
features.syntax: db.Users.Where(u => u.Id == id).ToListAsync()
limitations: The first-party provider is embedded/direct only. The proved query slice covers Where, ordering, Skip/Take, scalar projection, Single, simple and bounded-shape Any, Count/LongCount, non-decimal constant/parameter collection Contains, simple Include, bounded direct inner joins, selected string and temporal members, finite-double math functions, bounded exact-decimal comparisons and ordering, bounded scalar numeric aggregates, direct-column integer-distinct aggregates, and direct single-table grouped numeric aggregates. Distinct scalar aggregates require an optional pre-filter followed by one directly mapped nonnullable int-column projection and Distinct, then Count, LongCount, Sum, Min, or Max. Distinct Average, nullable or non-int columns, value converters, ordering, limits, predicates after Distinct, intervening operators, computed/composite selectors, casts, and derived sources are rejected. GroupBy requires direct mapped bool, integral, enum, default-BINARY string, or nullable keys; composite keys must use C# anonymous types or ValueTuple, and Boolean keys require canonical provider-written 0/1 storage. It supports one direct-key/bare-aggregate projection, qualified non-distinct Sum/Average/Min/Max, nonnullable-int direct-column distinct Count/LongCount/Sum/Min/Max, basic HAVING, and ordering by a directly projected key or aggregate. Double, transformed, non-BINARY-collated, or configured-converter keys; aggregate value converters; element/result selector overloads; group materialization; raw or post-projection group transforms; nested/joined grouping; predicate/CASE aggregates; and broader types/shapes are rejected. String StartsWith/EndsWith/instance Contains and StringComparison overloads, DateTimeOffset components, integral/decimal/MathF/precision-argument/midpoint-mode/transcendental math overloads, decimal arithmetic/aggregates/Contains, long- and float-valued Sum/Average/Min/Max variants and other unqualified aggregate variants, TakeWhile/SkipWhile, Concat/Union/Except/Intersect, ExecuteUpdate, filtered-inner/composite/chained/outer/cross joins, and correlated-query shapes remain outside the qualified surface. Recognized unsupported CLR methods, members, operators, aggregate shapes, join shapes, and decimal expressions fail before command dispatch with provider diagnostics.
deviations: ToLower()/ToUpper() and their invariant variants map to CSharpDB LOWER/UPPER and therefore use invariant server semantics rather than .NET CurrentCulture.

Math.Round(double) uses CSharpDB midpoint-to-even semantics. Scalar numeric aggregate qualification is limited to bounded values and the explicitly documented CLR types because engine accumulation and EF-generated conversions differ for wider variants.
Nullable Distinct().Count()/LongCount() is intentionally rejected because LINQ counts one distinct NULL while SQL COUNT(DISTINCT column) ignores NULL.

features.syntax: dotnet ef database update
limitations: The supported operation set is create/drop table, add/drop/rename column, rename table, create/drop/rename index, add/drop named check, unique, foreign-key, and qualified primary-key constraints, plus AlterColumn changes to literal defaults, nullability, exact dependency-free INTEGER/REAL types, and TEXT collations. TEXT collation changes atomically rebuild affected ready ordinary/unique SQL indexes that inherit the column collation; key/FK/full-text/collection dependencies remain unsupported. For numeric type changes the generator drops an old default before TYPE and reapplies a compatible target default afterward; indexed numeric changes remain unsupported because REAL index keys are not implemented. Compound AlterColumn command sequences must execute in one migration transaction. Adding a single INTEGER primary key rekeys validated populated rows and atomically rebuilds ready ordinary/unique SQL, constraint-internal, and foreign-key-internal indexes; full-text, collection, and non-ready indexes remain unsupported. TEXT/BLOB type conversions and broader dependency rewriting remain unsupported.

features.syntax: IMigrator.GenerateScript(options: Idempotent)
limitations: Scripts target file-backed embedded databases and the migration operations supported by the provider.

features.syntax: ANALYZE orders
limitations: Temporary tables are excluded in the current version.
deviations: ANALYZE collects planner statistics; it does not show or execute a query plan.

features.syntax: EXPLAIN ESTIMATE FOR SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42
limitations: Output describes estimate inputs and planner decisions. It follows read-only transaction routing and prepared commands discover/rebind parameters inside the target. It is not a complete physical operator tree and reports no actual execution metrics.
deviations: The target query is not executed; mutation targets are rejected.

features.syntax: EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM orders WHERE customer_id = 42
limitations: The runtime does not yet expose one shared physical-plan descriptor for every execution path.

features.syntax: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM orders
limitations: Per-operator actual rows, loops, timings, memory, and stop states are not instrumented or exposed.
